items.q: Which colours typically advance in a composition?
items.why: Students conflate darkness with advancement. In atmospheric perspective, COOLER colours recede (think distant blue mountains) and warmer colours advance, regardless of darkness.

items.q: What can line in a composition imply, even without representing an object?
items.correct: Movement, direction, emotion, and visual rhythm.
items.misconception: Only the outline of an object.
items.why: Students treat line as merely descriptive (an outline). In design, line is expressive - jagged lines suggest energy, curves softness, diagonals movement, even abstractly.

items.q: What did Renaissance artists rediscover that medieval art lacked?
items.correct: Linear perspective and naturalistic representation of the human body.
items.why: Students assume medieval art was colourless. It was vivid but flat - the Renaissance breakthrough was mathematical PERSPECTIVE (Brunelleschi) and anatomical realism, not the introduction of colour.

items.q: Whose 1936 Migrant Mother is FSA's defining image?
items.why: It's Dorothea Lange. “Walker Evans” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Whose Dusseldorf typologies trained Gursky and Struth?
items.why: Correct answer: Becher. “Sander” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Daguerre announce his process?
items.why: The real figure is 1839 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
